name: Charles Thompson
abstract: Charles Phillip Thompson (March 21, 1918 – June 16, 2016), known as Sir Charles Thompson, was an American swing and bebop pianist, organist, composer, and arranger.

concerts.label: Fred Robbins One-Nite Stand: Illinois Jacquet and His Orchestra with Ella Fitzgerald
JVC Jazz Festival: Sir Charles Thompson

about_quotes.speaker: Bud Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: all those people in the group called Jazz of the Swing Era, were in the Newport Jazz Festival too. Because we had Earl Hines and Charles Thompson, the two pianists.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: playing alto and clarinet, that you were first of all in the Hampton Sextet, a group which included pianist Sir Charles Thompson... Is that right? That's correct.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: It was J. J., Joe Newman, Sir Charles, Al Lucas, Shadow Wilson... I would augment you know -- they would get me to record with them on all of their records

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: They had Sir Charles Thompson on piano... And they came to work at Billy Berg's. So Howard decided to stay here... So he asked me... I went for it because I knew that he was in the vein closer to what I really wanted to be into.

about_quotes.speaker: Ram Ramirez
about_quotes.evidence: T-Bone and I used to ride down there, with Oscar Pettiford, Sir Charles Thompson, Red Nichols and his Pennies and Nat Cole... And (?) played was Coleman Hawkins, Sir Charles on piano, Denzil Best...

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: Sir Charles Thompson on piano, Marshall Royal, Ernie Royal, Ray Perry, the jazz violinist, and another fellow named Beckett who was quite a trombone player.
